statement.title: Statement
statement.content:
Description
![](https://cdn.luogu.com.cn/upload/vjudge_pic/CF2095B/230c6d6e9cafaad267dedda0065554461d8fcf5a.png)Can you win 10 times?

Input Format
The only line contains the text " $ \texttt{Game}~x $ ", where $ x $ is an integer between $ 1 $ and $ 10 $ (inclusive) — the number of the current round of the game.

Output Format
Output a single integer representing the number of the column you want to drop into.

Examples
Sample 1
Input
Game 1
Output
​​

Sample 2
Input
Game 2
Output
​

solutions.title: Solution
solutions.content:
In this problem you need to drop a ball from a column of your choosing and get it to the green area.
With simple analysis (similar to Pascal's triangle), one can notice that best chance of winning is by dropping the ball from column $$$0$$$ or $$$16$$$ where you get $$$\frac{1}{2}$$$ chance to win. This means expected number of plays to win $$$10$$$ times is $$$2^{10} = 1024$$$, which is a lot (technically though, it is $$$2^{9} = 512$$$ as the first game always wins for penalty reasons).
However, one can notice that the green area spans further than columns $$$0$$$ to $$$16$$$. This allows one to win simply by dropping the ball from any column outside that range.
If anyone got accepted by chance using columns $$$[0, 16]$$$, please write in comments.
Lastly, if you have not noticed already,
this
was a hint for this problem.
